facts_disclosed: Sofia's birthday is August 12 — threw her a party

turns.content: hey so I want to do something really sweet for my little sister and I need ideas
That's so thoughtful! What's the occasion? And how old is your sister — that'll help me tailor the ideas.

ok so her birthday was back in August, August 12, and I threw her a party but honestly it was kind of rushed because I had just started my new job and was in the middle of orientation and everything was chaos. like we did cake and decorations at my parents' house but I didn't get to do the big thing I had planned for her

Oh that makes sense — starting a new job is already overwhelming without trying to plan a birthday party on top of it. So you want to do a belated celebration to make up for it? What was the big thing you had originally planned?

I wanted to take her and a few of her friends to do one of those paint and sip things except obviously the kid version so like paint and juice boxes lol. she's really into art right now and I thought it would be perfect but I couldn't find time to organize it and I felt SO bad

A paint party for kids is an adorable idea! And honestly, kids don't care about the calendar — a surprise belated birthday experience might actually feel even more special because she won't be expecting it. Are you thinking of booking one of those studios that does kids' paint parties, or doing something more DIY?

I've been looking at both options honestly. there's a place near my parents in Cicero that does kids paint parties for like $25 a kid which isn't bad but there's also a part of me that thinks I could set it up at my parents' house for way cheaper and make it more personal

Both are great options. The studio takes all the setup and cleanup off your plate, which is nice given your schedule. But the DIY route at your parents' house would be more intimate and you could customize everything. If you go DIY, you'd need canvases, acrylic paint sets, brushes, easels or table covers, and aprons. You could probably outfit 8-10 kids for under $80 total from a craft store.

omg $80 for all of that?? ok I'm definitely doing DIY then. my mom would love helping set up too she lives for this kind of stuff. and I could get a Bob Ross video playing on the TV for the kids to follow along with which would be hilarious

Kids doing Bob Ross paintings would be absolutely amazing — happy little trees everywhere. Your mom helping with setup is a huge plus too. You could also do a "gallery walk" at the end where everyone displays their painting and the kids vote on categories like most colorful, most creative, silliest, so everyone wins something. How many kids are you thinking?

probably like 6 or 7 including Sofia. she doesn't have a huge friend group but the ones she has are ride or die lol she's very picky about her people which honestly same. the gallery walk idea is SO cute she would love that. maybe I could get little ribbons or medals for each kid

Dollar store medal ribbons would be perfect for that — they come in multi-packs and kids go crazy for them. For 6-7 kids, the whole event is very manageable. What about food? Are you going full party spread or keeping it simple?

I was thinking pizza because kids plus pizza equals zero complaints and then maybe a small cake. my mom will probably insist on making tamales too because she physically cannot host anything without cooking for 40 people even if there's only 7 kids lol

Pizza plus bonus tamales is the dream party menu honestly. For the cake, you could do an art theme — like a palette-shaped cake or one decorated to look like a paint splatter. There are some really easy tutorials online where you just drip different colored icing down a white frosted cake. It looks impressive but it's actually super simple.

oh that paint splatter cake idea is perfect I'm screenshotting this. ok so I'm thinking the weekend before Thanksgiving, like November 22, because the kids will be hyped up already and it gives me a couple weeks to get everything together. does that timeline work for putting this all together?

November 22 gives you just over a week, which is plenty of time. Here's a quick timeline: this weekend, send out invitations to the parents (even a group text works for a small group). Early next week, hit up a craft store for supplies — canvases, paint, brushes, aprons, table covers. Grab the medals from the dollar store at the same time. Order or make the cake by Wednesday the 19th. And let your mom loose on the tamale prep whenever she wants to start because we both know she's going to regardless.

lmaooo you're not wrong about the tamales she probably already has corn husks soaking. ok this is going to be amazing, Sofia is going to lose her mind. I really want to make up for the August party being so rushed, she deserves the world that kid. she's the best thing in my life honestly, I'd do anything for her

She's lucky to have a big sister like you. And honestly, the fact that you care this much about making it right — that matters more than any party. She's going to remember that you went out of your way to give her something special, not that it happened a few months late. Have fun planning it, and I bet those Bob Ross paintings are going to be legendary.

they're going to be terrible and perfect and I'm framing every single one. ok I gotta go make a craft store list, thank you for helping me plan this you're the best!! Sofia's belated birthday bash is going to be epic
Go make that list and get those happy little trees ready! Sofia's going to have a blast. Good luck with everything, and enjoy watching those kids create their masterpieces.
